~SANTA FOR PRESIDENT
: 
He grew up in a village without electricity. Today, he runs Africa’s largest emerging-tech community over 7,000 members across Ghana, Nigeria, Kenya, Togo, Sierra Leone, The Gambia, Zimbabwe, Cameroon, Liberia and Rwanda. By 2050, the network he leads expects to have impacted 1 billion young Africans. We are honoured to host Joshua Opoku Agyemang, President of the Ghana STEM Network and IoT Network Hub Africa, at the Ghana Investment Tour, Maiden Edition. Date: Friday, 3rd July 2026 Time: 9:00 AM Venue: Cedi Conference Centre, University of Ghana The best way to predict the future of Ghana is to build it. Come and hear it from the man already living that line. Register: https://git.createhubghana.org/events #GhanaInvestmentTour #panafricanism #OwnTheFuture #technology #financialindependence

NOTICE TO ALL GCTU CLASS REPRESENTATIVES CITSET, in collaboration with NEIP, invites all Class Representatives to an online meeting on the Students Entrepreneurship & Enterprise Development (SEED) Programme. The meeting is aimed at briefing Class Representatives on the programme and how interested students can benefit from the training, mentorship, networking opportunities, and funding support available. Date: Tomorrow, 20th june, 2026. Time: 11:00 AM Venue: Online : https://teams.live.com/meet/9336375580901?p=JG7LzH7Q1IUQcfEUNd All Class Representatives are kindly requested to attend and share the information with their respective classes. CITSET – GCTU In collaboration with NEIP.

DAY 1 IS HERE! THE ESA CAREERBOOST × HIKVISION BOOTCAMP KICKS OFF TODAY! ​The countdown is over! Today, we turn our core agenda into action: No engineering student must graduate with just an undergraduate certificate! It’s time to step into the lab, get hands-on, and build the market-ready skills that set you apart. ​ Where you need to be right now: Venue: CITSET Lab — Last floor of the Graduate Block Time: Doors are open! Sessions start exactly at 9:00 AM Sharp. We wait for no one! ​ Quick Reminder:  Make sure you have Hik-Partner Pro installed on your phone, and bring your notebook, a pen, and your networking tools if you have them. ​ Let’s lock in and level up, engineers!   ​#ESAGCTU #ESACareerBoost #HikvisionBootcamp #Day1 #SkillsOverDegrees @all

Desmond
6/3/2026
KEY HIGHLIGHTS FROM THE ODSA OPEN FORUM An open forum was held yesterday by the Dean of Students Affairs Office to engage students directly on key concerns across campus. The Acting Vice Chancellor, Deans, and Directors were present to listen and respond to issues raised by students, the following are key concerns raised by students and the responses from management. 1. Student Portal Blocks Over Unpaid Fees: Students raised concerns over blockage of SIP preventing access to past results as being unfairly. The Dean of Student Affairs explained that full payment is the official policy, and the 50% arrangement was only a grace period structure to reduce financial constraints on students. The block is a strategy to compel students to pay thier outstanding fees on time to support the university’s operations and on going projects. 2. Short Academic Calendar Duration: Students complained about the compressed semester affecting learning. The Dean of Students Affairs and the Dean of FOCIS agreed and announced there will be a managerial meeting within the next two days to review the academic calendar and consider restoring and extending the weeks to ensure the standard academic week structure. 3. Delay in Release of FOCIS Results: The Dean of FOCIS explained that results were intentionally held to fix the grading errors and missing marks in last semester results before release. This is to ensure students do not face post release corrections. 4.Missing Grades and Result Issues fixing: The Dean of FOCIS has introduced a new system to address exam inconsistencies before results are released. Students with past results issues are to visit the faculty for rectification forms or their HODs for immediate fixing of their results issues as directed by the Deans present. Final year students were assured they will graduate on time, and students who may need special resits where necessary will be provided. 5. Student ID Cards and Portal Photos: If an entire class has missing IDs, the class rep should report it. Individual cases should go to the ICT Directorate. Students must also ensure a clear passport photo is uploaded on their portal for ID processing. 6. Lectures And Teaching Assistant: Prof Kester stated that Teaching Assistants must not exceed 40% of course delivery. Any overstepping should be reported to the faculty. Students were also informed that some instructors who may seem to be TAs on campus are contracted professionals(Adjunct lecturers), not TAs. 7. Classroom and Hostel Conditions: Concerns were raised about bad classroom conditions including broken ceilings in C14 and C15, faulty ACs, and hostel bed conditions. The Head of Works and Housing promised that ceilings, ACs (including the business campus), and campus hostel beds will be fixed and replaced within this month. 8. Opened Study Halls and Classroom Wi-Fi: C9 and C14 will remain open after lectures for all students who want to study and other rooms will be opened if demands go higher. Prof Kester noted these rooms will be equipped with free Wi-Fi  within this month to support students group and personal learning. 9. Final Year Transcript Audits: Prof Kester announced that all Level 400 and Diploma 200 transcripts will be audited early to correct results errors and prevent graduation delays, while ensuring accountability for outstanding result issues. 10. Campus Internet, Certification Courses and Laptop Packages: The director of ITSS stated the school is partnering with Celltel to improve internet strength across both campuses. Their office is also available to support students with IT issues and providing resources students are entitled to and, in collaboration with SRC, introduce professional IT certification courses and installment-based laptop packages for students. 11. Lecture Hours (1:30 Model) concerns: The Dean of FOCIS explained the adoption of the 1 hour 30 minutes lecture model instead of the original 3 hour lecture format is influenced by the inadequate infrastructure and to maintain all lectures are face to fa
